headline: Korean AI chip startup DEEPX, Hyundai work on robots powered by generative AI
summary: South Korean AI chip startup DEEPX will expand its partnership with Hyundai Motor Group to develop a computing platform for generative ‌AI robots using its second generation of low-power chips, its top executive said, as ‌it gets set for an IPO.  DEEPX is in talks with the government and investors to raise more than 600 ​billion won ($408 million) in an ongoing funding round, in the run-up to a potential IPO in South Korea, DEEPX CEO Lokwon Kim said.  The company, which began producing chips late last year, is one of a number of South Korean startups riding on Seoul's ambitions to nurture artificial intelligence champions and ‌make the country an AI leader.

headline: Apple Lawsuit Puts Apple Pay Controls And Services Valuation In Focus
summary: Apple has been named as a defendant in a wide reaching lawsuit alleging failure to prevent high frequency gambling transactions processed via Apple Pay. The case centers on claims that Apple, alongside other financial and e commerce firms, enabled hundreds of rapid gambling payments tied to an allegedly fraudulent gaming operator. Plaintiffs are seeking substantial damages and injunctive relief, raising questions about Apple Pay’s consumer protection controls and regulatory compliance. For...

headline: The Bezos Vs. Musk Space Race Is Heating Up
summary: Bezos-founded companies are pushing ahead in satellite connections and rocket launches, businesses that Musk’s SpaceX has long dominated.

headline: Amazon’s $11.6 Billion Globalstar Deal Amps Up Musk Rivalry
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Amazon.com Inc.’s $11.6 billion deal to buy Globalstar Inc. ramps up competition with Elon Musk’s Starlink in satellite communications, a market predicted to double to $200 billion in the coming years.On Tuesday, Amazon announced plans to buy the satellite operator for a mix of stock and up to $90 a share and said that in 2028 it will launch a direct-to-device service, offering connectivity straight to people’s mobile devices as an alternative to traditional cell towers.Through Gl

headline: Why Amazon Is Buying Globalstar—and What It Means for Other Satellite Companies
summary: Globalstar  stock jumped on Tuesday after  Amazon.com  agreed to buy the satellite company, in a move that could have big implications for the wider space sector.  Amazon said it would buy Globalstar for the equivalent of $90 a share.  Globalstar shareholders can elect to sell their shares for cash or receive the online retailer’s stock at a ratio of 0.3210 Amazon shares per Globalstar share, with a value capped at $90.00 per share.

headline: Apple CEO Tim Cook Just Bought Nike Stock. His Last Purchase Didn’t Go So Well.
summary: Cook is Nike’s lead independent director and has been on the company board since 2005.  Cook bought $3 million worth of shares in Nike at the end of December.  After the purchase, Cook holds 130,480 Nike shares, worth about $5.7 million based on Tuesday’s closing price.
